user_message: Working on Parnell Aerospace's deployment blockers. Got the meeting transcript -- help me draft the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Parnell Aerospace
Date: 2024-02-07
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Parnell Aerospace)

[00:02:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on deployment blockers from our last session.

[00:05:00] David (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:07:00] David (Parnell Aerospace): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:10:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:16:00] David (Parnell Aerospace): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-B trap (Actions without owner); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-B failure mode (Actions without owner)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-B -- Actions without owner
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
